title: RsaKey::generate casts c_int key size to u32 without range check
description: wolfhsm_rsa_get_size writes the key size into a c_int (signed 32-bit). After the rc==0 success check, the code unconditionally casts out_size (c_int) to u32 via 'out_size as u32'. If the server returns 0 or a negative value (e.g., due to a server bug or unexpected state), the cast produces 0 or a very large u32 (up to 2^32-1). raw_op then allocates vec\![0u8; self.key_size_bytes as usize] with that unchecked size, causing OOM or a zero-length output buffer. Fix: validate out_size > 0 after the rc check and before storing it.
